title: Miltin Takel
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}}
{{Character

|birth=

As ''The Essential Reader's Companion'' dates both novels to [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]], ''The Glove of Darth Vader'' must also take place in 5 ABY.</ref> [[Kessel/Legends|Kessel]]<ref name="Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals" />

{{Quote|Certainly you must be joking, Grand Admiral Takel. A scandal years ago involving the late Grand Moff Tarkin and a female aide? I'm sure today's Imperial administration is quite above such improprieties. Oh, and yes, I'll have a package for you next week.|Wendell Wright-Sims|<ref name="Shadows of the Empire Sourcebook">{{WEGCite|story=An Old Flame in Need...|book=Shadows of the Empire Sourcebook}}</ref>}}
'''Miltin Takel''' was a veteran [[Fleet Admiral/Legends|fleet admiral]] who became one of the twelve original [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admirals]] appointed by [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] [[2 BBY/Legends|two years before]] the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. Known for his strong vices, the Grand Admiral was a [[Spice/Legends|spice]] addict from the [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Gargon/Legends|Gargon]] and used the resources of the Empire to obtain the highest-quality substances. In addition, Takel had a lust for [[Alien/Legends|non-Human]] women, something that would have been looked down upon by the Imperials who subscribed to [[High Human Culture/Legends|Human High Culture]] had Takel's brother, [[Governor/Legends|Imperial Governor]] [[Griff Takel]], not hid the Grand Admiral's improprieties. Despite these proclivities, Takel was a masterful commander, aided by the limited [[Telepathy/Legends|telepathy]] that was granted to him by the use of [[Glitterstim/Legends|glitterstim]].

Following the [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor's]] [[Death/Legends|death]] during the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], Takel's influence quickly slipped away. The opposing Grand Admiral [[Josef Grunger]] used the Imperial fallout from [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] to bully Takel's forces out of his former holdings, causing a minor power struggle that ended with Takel's retreat. Desperate to get the better of Grunger, Takel fell in with the [[Supreme Slavelord|Slavelord]] [[Trioculus]] and the [[Central Committee of Grand Moffs]], who promised to deal with the upstart Grand Admiral. However, the alliance proved to be Takel's downfall. In a state of spice-induced paranoia, Takel questioned the [[Slavery/Legends|slavelord]] on his plan to take the Imperial throne. Tired of the Grand Admiral undermining his authority, Trioculus had Takel executed by his right-hand man, [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Bertroff Hissa|Hissa]], with a single [[Laser/Legends|blast]] from his [[Blaster pistol/Legends|laser pistol]].

==Biography==
===Grand Admiral===
{{Quote|For extreme heroism, and for not relinquishing your ship, even when faced with the latest and most heinous example of Rebel terrorism since the destruction of Alderaan, I present you with this, the Distinguished Medal of Imperial Honor.|Grand Admiral Miltin Takel presents an award to Captain Sergus Lanox|<ref name="The Capture of Imperial Hazard">{{JournalCite|10|The Capture of Imperial Hazard|The Capture of ''Imperial Hazard''}}</ref>}}
Native to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Gargon/Legends|Gargon]] in the [[Mandalore sector/Legends|Mandalore sector]], a world known for its [[Spice/Legends|spice]] production, the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] Miltin Takel was a flamboyant, street-talking man with a taste for hobnobbing with the upper crust of society who loved to spread rumors and gossip among those who lived in the lap of luxury. Takel was well known for his carnal weaknesses, enjoying the pleasure of both spice and women, usually at the same [[Time/Legends|time]]. However, only those persons close to the man knew that his passions fell outside the Imperial ideals of [[High Human Culture/Legends|Human High Culture]]. Takel's penchant for females of other [[Species/Legends|species]] was kept secret by his brother,<ref name="Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals" /> [[Griff Takel]], the [[Governor/Legends|Governor]] of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core World]] [[Esseles/Legends|Esseles]], who had been promoted to that position during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] in [[21 BBY/Legends|21 BBY]].<ref name="Shadowfeed" /> The influence of his brother allowed Takel to pursue his depraved interests with impunity.<ref name="Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals" />

Despite these issues, Takel possessed a keen strategic mind, which he augmented with [[Glitterstim/Legends|glitterstim]],<ref name="Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals" /> a spice that provided [[Telepathy/Legends|mind-reading abilities]].<ref name="Underworld">{{InsiderCite|89|Underworld: A Galaxy of Scum and Villainy}}</ref> By the time of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], Takel's ties to the Imperial elite allowed him to procure the highest-quality spices. [[Wendell Wright-Sims]], an importer with express permission from [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] to provide spice to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant's]] elite, was Takel's personal [[Drug dealer|spice dealer]].<ref name="Shadows of the Empire Sourcebook" /> Takel's spice habit gave him an edge that he needed to achieve victory at the [[Siege of Trasemene]].<ref name="Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals" /> This victory was highly celebrated by the Empire, and its inclusion in [[Plescinia Entertainments/Legends|Plescinia Entertainments]]' [[CS-Mark 10]] Imperial Command Combat Simulator was one of only twelve historical battles to be placed within the [[Flight simulator/Legends|simulator's]] "Great Battles of the Empire" series.<ref name="Galladinium's Fantastic Technology">''[[Galladinium's Fantastic Technology]]''</ref>

[[File:Vadergrandadmirals.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Darth Vader presents Emperor Palpatine, Miltin Takel, and Thrawn with plans for the second Death Star.]]
By [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]], Takel had built a reputation as a highly noted Imperial commander,<ref name="Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals" /> who by then had [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of service as a [[Fleet Admiral/Legends|fleet admiral]].<ref name="EGTW">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref> As such, he was one of only twelve commanders promoted to the newly created rank of [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] in an elaborate ceremony during Coruscant's [[Fete Week|New Year Fete Week]]. This promotion also included a [[Grand Admiral's uniform/Legends|change in uniform]].<ref name="Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals" /> During his time as a Grand Admiral, Takel's [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] was the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] ''[[Magic Dragon]]''.<ref name="BT:AHotE">{{SW|url=news/barely-tolerable-alien-henchmen-of-the-empire-part-2|series=Barely Tolerable: Alien Henchmen of the Empire|text=Part 2|nolive=1}}</ref> In [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], Takel was one of several [[Imperial officer/Legends|officers]] to make an appearance on Coruscant with the [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] and [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] for New Year Fete Week.<ref name="Galaxywide NewsNets 5">{{JournalCite|5|Galaxywide NewsNets}}</ref> Around the same time, Takel was involved in a ceremony in which he awarded the [[Distinguished Medal of Imperial Honor/Legends|Distinguished Medal of Imperial Honor]] to [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Sergus Lanox]] for keeping his [[Victory-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]], the ''[[Imperial Hazard]]'', out of [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] hands. This ceremony was important enough to be broadcast over the [[HoloNet/Legends|HoloNet]].<ref name="The Capture of Imperial Hazard" />

After [[Battle of the City of Bone|eradicating]] [[Slaver/Legends|slavers]] operating on the planet [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]]<ref name="The History of the Mandalorians">{{InsiderCite|80|The History of the Mandalorians: Exploring the Evolution of a Mighty Race of Warriors}}</ref> in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] people rallied behind their leader, [[Fenn Shysa]], and rose up against Takel's forces, whose territory included his home Mandalore [[Sector/Legends|sector]].<ref name="The History of the Mandalorians">{{InsiderCite|80|The History of the Mandalorians: Exploring the Evolution of a Mighty Race of Warriors}}</ref> Eventually, the rejuvenated [[Mandalorian Protectors (Spar)|Mandalorian Protectors]] were able to oust the Grand Admiral from Mandalorian [[Realspace/Legends|space]].<ref name="Galaxy at War">''[[Galaxy at War]]''</ref> Despite this unrest, Takel did not abandon his social engagements. He was one of a select few who were invited to the premier of ''[[The Kallea Cycle]]'' opera on Imperial Center in 3 ABY. At a gala commemorating the opening night of this performance, the subject of the Imperial defeat at the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] was still fresh in the minds of the patrons, and Takel was heard spreading a rumor of the late [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Wilhuff Tarkin's]] improprieties against his [[Thalassa Tarkin|wife]] with a [[Natasi Daala|female aide]].<ref name="Shadows of the Empire Sourcebook" /> At some point after the [[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Mitth'raw'nuruodo]] took the place of the traitorous [[Demetrius Zaarin]] among the Grand Admirals, following the latter's [[Zaarin insurrection|insurrection]] against the Emperor<ref name="Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals" /> in 3.5 ABY,<ref name="NEC" /> Takel joined Thrawn at a meeting with [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Vader and Palpatine, whey they witnessed a [[Hologram/Legends|holographic]] readout of the under-construction [[Death Star II/Legends|Death Star II]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]].<ref name="Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals" />

===The downfall of the Empire===
{{Quote|You can't do this, Trioculus! You're being too hasty. Until you find the glove of Darth Vader, you won't be accepted as the new Emperor. What if Grand Admiral Grunger finds it first and—|Grand Admiral Miltin Takel's last words|<ref name="The Glove of Darth Vader" />}}
The unrest in the Mandalore sector did not prevent Takel from being called away to the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]] to take part in the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]. Alongside several other Grand Admirals, including [[Nial Declann]], [[Afsheen Makati]], and [[Osvald Teshik]], Takel was stationed aboard the second [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]] to witness the Empire's final destruction of the Rebel Alliance. During the battle, Takel was intoxicated with glitterstim, a decision that would ultimately save his life. The drug's ability to give the user telepathic abilities allowed Takel to sense the unease radiating from Declann, who was busy helping to coordinate the Imperial [[Fleet/Legends|Fleet]] with [[Battle meditation/Legends|battle meditation]]. Not waiting to determine the cause of his contemporary's fears, Takel fled the [[Space station/Legends|station]], narrowly escaping the Death Star's destruction. Rather than attempt to rally the Fleet as Teshik did, Takel fled the ensuing battle, as did Makati.<ref name="Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals" />

Following this defeat, Takel's fortunes rapidly deteriorated. Grand Admiral [[Josef Grunger]], who was busy aiding Takel's forces in the search for "[[Nagai/Legends|albino-skinned marauders]]"<ref name="Forgotten War">{{Hyperspace|url=fans/hyperspace/source/nagai11202009/index.html|text=The Forgotten War: The Nagai and the Tofs|int=The Forgotten War: The Nagai and the Tofs}}</ref> who had been reported near Takel's [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of Gargon, decided to take advantage of the death of the Emperor by making a bid for the throne.<ref name="Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals" /> While Takel continued to defend his base of operations while awaiting orders from Palpatine's successor,<ref name="EGTW" /> Grunger opened fire on Takel's [[Executive officer/Legends|first officer]] immediately after hearing of the defeat at Endor, disabling [[Unidentified Imperial first officer|his]] [[Starship/Legends|ship]] and taking control of the planet. Using his high rank and the sense of confusion surrounding the Emperor's death, Grunger quickly bullied Takel's forces out of Gargon.<ref name="Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals" />

[[File:Central_Committee.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Trioculus meets with his loyal followers.]]
In the aftermath of the loss of his home sector, Takel attached his banner to [[Director of Imperial Intelligence]] [[Ysanne Isard]] and attempted to retake his former holdings. However, Grunger's immense armada caused the campaign to end in failure. After Isard threatened to execute Takel for this defeat, the Grand Admiral decided to barter his allegiance to other upstarts who had made a bid for the throne. In this capacity, Takel fled to the convocation of Grand [[Moff/Legends|Moffs]] on [[Kessel/Legends|Kessel]], where the [[Central Committee of Grand Moffs]] presented the [[Supreme Slavelord|Slavelord]] [[Trioculus]] as the Son of Palpatine and the rightful heir to the throne. In a spice-induced haze, Takel somehow felt connected to the three-eyed [[Slavery/Legends|slavelord]]. The feeling of connection was reinforced when Trioculus promised to deal with the upstart Grunger.<ref name="Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals" />

In [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]],<ref name="TERC" /> with a passing attachment to Trioculus' cause, Takel was present at a closed meeting whose attendance included Grand Moff [[Bertroff Hissa|Hissa]] and a select group of Grand Moffs loyal to Trioculus. The group indulged in a meal of [[Whaladon/Legends|Whaladon]] meat before Trioculus addressed the small group. The slavelord demanded that the group use all their resources to retrieve the [[glove of Darth Vader]], based on a proclamation by the false [[Supreme Prophet]] [[Rajah Ubooki|Kadann]] that only the owner of the glove would receive his blessing as the new Emperor. Following this, Trioculus asked for suggestions for a new home base. Takel offered the world of [[Dagobah/Legends|Dagobah]] as a suggestion, but the idea was quickly dismissed by the slavelord.<ref name="The Glove of Darth Vader" />

Despite other suggestions, Trioculus decided on the [[Water/Legends|ice]] planet of [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]] as a new base. However, Takel, on another of his spice binges, suddenly rose from the table to question Trioculus' order that all available equipment be shipped to Hoth. Paranoid over the vulnerability that this operation would put them in, the Grand Admiral became irate, claiming that Trioculus was being too hasty in his plans and that if Grunger found the glove, any future plans would be doomed. Before Takel could continue his rant, he was quickly executed by Hissa with a [[Laser/Legends|shot]] from his sidearm [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]] at Trioculus' unspoken order. At his death, Takel fell forward onto the meeting room's banquet table, a victim of Trioculus' bid for power.<ref name="The Glove of Darth Vader" />

The circumstances of Takel's death were later covered in the [[Historical Council|New Republic Historical Council]] document ''[[The Essential Chronology]]'',<ref name="EC">[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']]</ref> released just prior to the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]] of [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]], and [[The New Essential Chronology (in-universe)|''The New Essential Chronology'']], an updated edition released in [[36 ABY]].<ref name="NEC" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|You're wasting my time!|Trioculus, on Grand Admiral Miltin Takel's suggestion for a new home base|<ref name="The Glove of Darth Vader" />}}
Grand Admiral Takel was a brilliant<ref name="Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals" /> and respected strategist whose promotion to Grand Admiral brooked little argument.<ref name="EGTW" /> However he was ultimately a slave to his many vices. His lust for the finer things made him a common presence at important Imperial events. Despite mingling with higher society, he did not abandon his less-savory aspects, continuing to talk in a street-wise manner and employing the services of [[Alien/Legends|non-Human]] females.<ref name="Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals" /> [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] dancers and [[Zeltron/Legends|Zeltron]] [[cafarel]] frequently shared Takel's private quarters aboard the ''Magic Dragon'' and the Grand Admiral was known to share his clothing with his non-Human companions.<ref name="BT:AHotE" /> Takel's addiction to spice was both a blessing and a curse. Although it allowed him a great victory at Trasemene and prevented him from perishing aboard the second Death Star, frequent use caused him to become quite paranoid. The paranoia caused by the drug led him to question Trioculus' orders too often, ultimately resulting in his demise<ref name="Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals" /> when he displayed a lack of tact during an important meeting.<ref name="The Glove of Darth Vader" />

Takel acquitted himself with his sense of style and was infamous for his flamboyancy. He enjoyed socializing with the Empire's social elite at high-profile events,<ref name="Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals" /> and he attended at least two New Year Fete Week events<ref name="Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals" /><ref name="Galaxywide NewsNets 5" /> and the opening premier of ''The Kallea Cycle''.<ref name="Shadows of the Empire Sourcebook" /> Additionally, he presented the Distinguished Medal of Imperial Honor to worthy candidates, as he did with Captain Sergus Lanox.<ref name="The Capture of Imperial Hazard" />

==Equipment==
To hold his spice supplies, Takel employed a [[Gundark/Legends|gundark]]-hide bag. He wore a white [[Grand Admiral's uniform/Legends|Grand Admiral's uniform]].<ref name="Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals" /> The Star Destroyer ''Magic Dragon'' served as Takel's flagship during his time as Grand Admiral.<ref name="BT:AHotE" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:SWAJ5.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Grand Admiral Miltin Takel was introduced in ''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 5.]]
Grand Admiral Takel was created for the ''[[Star Wars Adventure Journal]]'' and was first mentioned in the ''[[Galaxywide NewsNets]]'' section of [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 5|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 5]] in [[February]] [[1995]], in which he was noted to have attended a New Year Fete Week celebration alongside Darth Vader and Emperor Palpatine.<ref name="Galaxywide NewsNets 5" /> Takel first appeared, as a hologram, once more in the ''Adventure Journal'', when he was mentioned in the short story ''[[The Capture of Imperial Hazard]]'' in [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 10|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 10]].<ref name="The Capture of Imperial Hazard" />

The gaming magazine ''[[Star Wars Gamer]]'' was initially slated to release an article entitled ''[[Dirty Dozen: The Imperial Grand Admirals]]'' by [[Daniel Wallace]] and [[Abel G. Peña]], which was set to give new information on the Grand Admirals, including Takel.<ref name="blog">{{Blog|danwallace|16|Grand Admirals: What Have They Got in their Pocketsses?}}</ref><ref name="document">"[[Dirty Dozen: The Imperial Grand Admirals]]"</ref> However, the magazine was canceled before the article could be released. The article was eventually [[Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals|picked up]] by the ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'' magazine and released in [[Star Wars Insider 66|''Star Wars Insider'' 66]] in [[2003]].<ref name="Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals" /><ref name="blog" /> The article revealed Takel's first name of Miltin and clarified that "Governor Takel" from ''Star Wars Adventure Journal 9'' was actually his brother.<ref name="document" /> Additionally, the article [[Retroactive continuity|retconned]] Takel as the unnamed Grand Admiral from Gargon who appeared in ''[[The Glove of Darth Vader]]''.<ref name="The Glove of Darth Vader" /><ref name="document" /> The cut content from the article initially prepared for ''Star Wars Gamer'' included [[roleplaying game]] stats and other additional information. Among this information was the name of Takel's personal [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]], ''Magic Dragon'',<ref name="blog" /><ref name="document" /> a reference to his drug habit.<ref>{{JCFCite|url=posts/12166958|board=Authors and Artists|thread=The Official Daniel Wallace Discussion Thread|user=[[Daniel Wallace|Dan Wallace]]|date=2003-06-25|time=5:53 AM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20180330121728/http://boards.theforce.net/threads/the-official-daniel-wallace-discussion-thread.5863946/page-5#post-12166958}}</ref> ''Magic Dragon'' was not confirmed as the name of Takel's Star Destroyer until the release of ''[[Barely Tolerable: Alien Henchmen of the Empire]]'' in [[2013]], which also repeated the Grand Admiral's previously cut liaisons with Twi'lek and Zeltron women aboard his flagship.<ref name="BT:AHotE" /><ref name="document" />

As explained by Abel G. Peña on the [[Jedi Council Forums]], in the article ''Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals'', he intended Miltin Takel to be the Grand Admiral shocked with [[Force lightning/Legends|lightning]] by Trioculus in ''The Glove of Darth Vader''. However, due to a miscommunication with illustrator [[Joe Corroney]], Corroney drew Takel with the appearance of another Imperial seen in a later meeting with Trioculus. <sup>([[:File:Central_Committee.jpg|see image, far left]])</sup> As it is, which Grand Admiral was in this scene remains ambiguous. It may be Takel, with some artistic license assumed in the illustration, or it may be an entirely different one; Peña has proposed [[Rufaan Tigellinus]] as a possibility.<ref>{{JCFCite|url=posts/26305709|board=Literature|thread=Grand Admirals and Emperor's Pawns articles info|user=[[Abel G. Peña|Halagad_Ventor]]|date=2007-03-05|time=6:22 PM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160405010850/http://boards.theforce.net/threads/grand-admirals-and-emperors-pawns-articles-info.26295788/#post-26305709}}</ref> The Grand Admiral who is shocked by Trioculus is not identified as being from Gargon, so it is possible that this man is separate from the Grand Admiral from Gargon who is executed by Hissa later in the book, as suggested.<ref name="The Glove of Darth Vader" />

{{Cutstart}}
===Cut content===
{{Quote|It's like being able to see out of a third eye, man. Real prime.|Miltin Takel, from the cut content of ''Dirty Dozen: Imperial Grand Admirals''|<ref name="Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals" />}}
Grand Admiral Takel had an unexplained ability to predict the maneuvers of the enemy, using his Star Destroyer, ''Magic Dragon''. Some in his forces believed Takel to be somewhat [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]], though most believed that it was due to the telepathic abilities granted by his addiction to glitterstim. When Takel evacuated the second Death Star, he boarded the Star Destroyer [[Chimaera/Legends|''Chimaera'']]. He was able to speak [[Rodese/Legends|Rodese]] and understand [[Lekku/Legends|Lekku]]. Upon his death, the last thought that went through Takel's mind was, "Prime."<ref name="document" />
{{Cutend}}
